description:
No spec subject — flake observation log, cause unknown.

Two uncaptured one-off failures on 2026-07-02 in worktree specled_ex-tracer-merge-on-flush (branch specled_-ia9/tracer-merge-on-flush), both green on immediate re-run and unreproducible since:
1. Full mix test run by the S2 verifier agent: 1 failure attributed to test/specled_ex/compiler/tracer_test.exs:255 (the merge_edges stream_data property, which is a pure function of generated inputs with invariants that hold by construction). Output lost to tail -5. 0/58 reproduction attempts (40 tagged-only seeded, 15 whole-file seeded, 3 full-suite seeded) plus verifier's 0/11.
2. mix spec.check merged tagged-tests subprocess run (first S3 acceptance run): verification_command_failed on the merged run affecting specled.decisions/tagged_tests/verification covers — same finding shape as the CI failure earlier that day (verifier_test.exs pgkill test on ubuntu), but on macOS where that test is otherwise stable. failing-tests line lost to a narrow grep. 5 subsequent spec.check runs green.

Both occurred under heavy load contexts (full suite / merged run). If either recurs: capture FULL output (the merged-run finding includes 'failing tests:' via attribution; ExUnit prints the seed at run start). Suspects to rule out with real evidence, not speculation: verifier_test timeout/pgkill tests under load, tracer_test real-manifest mutation interplay, resource exhaustion.

comments.text: Root cause found for observation 2 (S3 verifier, deterministic repro with full output captured): the merged tagged-tests child VM inherits the parent environment, and spec_check_test.exs's SPECLED_SHOW_INFO env-mutation tests are order-dependent under a leaked/exported var — test :11 (default run suppresses :info) fails seed-dependently because another test's after block deletes the var VM-globally, so intra-file order decides. Not a defect in the ia9 commits. Candidate fixes when actioned: (a) spec_check_test setup should snapshot+restore SPECLED_SHOW_INFO (and assert a clean baseline by deleting it in setup), (b) the verifier's merged-run command could sanitize SPECLED_* vars from the child env the same way the tagged-tests formatter consumes SPECLED_ATTRIBUTION_PATH. Observation 1 (tracer property one-off) remains unexplained and unreproduced.

Third observation, 2026-07-03, during specled_-7md.3 rebase-worker verification (worktree specled_ex-subject-detail-pivots, branch specled_-7md.3/subject-detail-pivots after rebase onto specled_-7md/spec-review-redesign at 706cdbb). Committed end-state is clean (5dc8707, spec.check status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, confirmed twice), but 4 consecutive mix spec.check runs on that same clean tree were non-deterministic:
- Run 1 (regen): status=pass errors=0 warnings=0
- Run 2: status=fail errors=1 warnings=0 — specled.compiler_tracer verification_command_failed, test/specled_ex/compiler/tracer_test.exs:241 (passes in isolation, 21/21)
- Run 3: status=fail errors=0 warnings=225
- Run 4: status=pass errors=0 warnings=0

Same shape as observation 1 (tracer_test one-off under full-suite/merged load) plus a new symptom not previously logged: run 3's spurious 225-warning spike, which doesn't match any known real finding count for this repo state and looks like a verification-rerun artifact rather than a genuine validation result. Not investigated further — orchestrator proceeded with the epic merge since the deterministic/cache-warm state is genuinely clean and this is unrelated to specled_-7md's work (spec.review only).

Fourth observation, 2026-07-03, during UI-polish follow-up work on specled_-7md's epic worktree (specled_ex-spec-review-redesign, branch specled_-7md/spec-review-redesign, uncommitted html.ex CSS/rendering changes only). Full mix test: 1 failure — test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs:1449 "a timeout with an empty artifact reports likely compile cost". Isolated re-run of verifier_test.exs alone: 41/41 pass. Full-suite re-run immediately after: 930/930 pass. Unrelated to the changes in flight (html.ex/CSS only, no verifier.ex touched). Same shape as prior observations — order/load-dependent flake in full-suite runs, clean in isolation and on retry.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: specled_-f98-flaky-test-hardening (implement, /implement)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/

Commit: 2a90f74
Files touched:
  - test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
  - test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs
Tests added: none — hardened two existing tests (no new behavior)
Verification stubs flipped: none (test-only; no spec change)
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean);
  mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs (12/12); mix test
  test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs (41/41); mix format --check-formatted
  (both files); mix spec.check --no-run-commands (status=pass errors=0
  warnings=0, exit 0 — 3 branch_guard findings downgraded to :info by
  Spec-Drift trailers). Full-suite proof deferred to the merge gate / PR CI.
Final-tier runs: 1
Deferred must requirements: none (ticket has no spec subject / no Advances)

Scope decision (confirmed with user): this ticket is a flake-observation log
with no verification contract. The elixir-test-critic audited the three named
observations. Two are genuine test-quality defects with proven mechanisms;
the third is a sound property (no fix warranted). Implemented the two fixes,
left observation 1/3 logged.

Fix 1 (observation 4) — verifier_test.exs:1436 "a timeout with an empty
artifact reports likely compile cost": budget 300ms -> 2000ms. The child must
reach its first line (`: > $SPECLED_ATTRIBUTION_PATH`) to create the empty
artifact; under load the three-level spawn loses that race against a 300ms
process-group SIGKILL, so read_artifact returns :absent instead of {:ok, []}
and the classified message flips off "likely compile cost". Both sibling
timeout tests already use 2000ms for the same reason. Product code is correct.

Fix 2 (observation 2) — spec_check_test.exs SPECLED_SHOW_INFO hermeticity:
added a module `setup` that deletes the VM-global var for a clean per-test
baseline and restores the prior value on exit (the old mutation test's `after`
deleted rather than restored, and no baseline was asserted). Dropped the now
redundant local try/after. Deterministic repro captured: with
SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1 exported, the pre-fix module FAILS test :11 at seed 0
([INFO] append_only/no_baseline leaks onto stdout, refute fails); the fixed
module passes at seed 0 and with the var exported across seeds.

Observation 1/3 (tracer merge_edges stream_data property, tracer_test.exs:241):
critic verdict — sound property, all four invariants hold universally, no
shared state / no manifest mutation. Most likely a timeout artifact under load
(100 iterations overrunning the slot), not a real counterexample. No code fix;
left logged for future seed capture if it recurs.

Summary: Hardened two order/load-dependent ExUnit flakes named in this ticket
(a too-tight timeout budget racing a child spawn, and a non-hermetic VM-global
env mutation) with the critic-recommended idiomatic fixes. Test-only; no
behavioral contract of any impacted subject moves, recorded via Spec-Drift:
test_only + branch_guard_missing_decision_update=info trailers.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 28e5109a-ac0e-4585-874a-bc4fdd50d0c3
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/28e5109a-ac0e-4585-874a-bc4fdd50d0c3.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs)
- Product (lib/) files touched: 0
- Fix 1 budget 300->2000, siblings confirmed at 2000: yes (verifier_test.exs sibling timeout tests at lines 1406/1459/1512 all use command_timeout_ms: 2000; changed test at 1436 now matches)
- Fix 2 setup composes with root context: yes (SpecLedEx.Case CaseTemplate setup returns {:ok, root: root}; new module setup returns :ok — both callbacks merge, root stays in context; all 12 tests still bind %{root: root})

Semantic checks:
- Targeted tests: spec_check_test pass 12/12, verifier_test pass 41/41
- Compile warnings-as-errors: clean
- Format: ok (both files)
- spec.check --no-run-commands: status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, exit 0 (3 branch_guard findings all downgraded to :info)
- Fix 2 counterfactual independently reproduced: pre-fix seed 0 FAIL (test :11, leaked [INFO] append_only/no_baseline on stdout, refute fails); fixed seed 0 PASS (SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1 exported both runs). Tree restored clean at 2a90f74.
- Spec-Drift trailers honest + accepted vocabulary: yes. test_only is a preset token (downgrades branch_guard_missing_subject_update -> both subject-update findings); branch_guard_missing_decision_update=info is a valid <code>=<severity> pair (info is an accepted severity token) matching the third finding — the tool's own fix hint recommends exactly this trailer. Change is test-only (0 lib files), so no behavioral contract of any impacted subject moves.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)

Verdict: PASS

Gaps (if FAIL): none
Notes: Observation 1/3 (tracer merge_edges stream_data property, tracer_test.exs) correctly left as-is per the ticket contract — no product or test change attempted for it. Both fixes are strictly test-quality (a too-tight timeout budget and a non-hermetic VM-global env mutation); no lib/ product behavior altered.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: specled_-f98-flaky-test-hardening (implement orchestrator)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/

TEST_CRITIC: PASS
Critic session: a6c6fdf2f8caed7d8 (cold, changed-files only)
Blocking findings: 0 (resolved: 0, waived: 0)
Files reviewed: test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs

Reasoning: Cold VERIFY reported PASS with zero product-file changes, zero scope
violations, passing targeted verification (compile clean, spec_check_test 12/12,
verifier_test 41/41, format ok, spec.check --no-run-commands exit 0), and an
independently reproduced Fix 2 counterfactual (pre-fix seed 0 FAILS, fixed seed 0
PASSES). The elixir-test-critic reviewed the diff cold and found the changes sound
with no new test-quality defect. This ticket carries no spec subject / no must
requirements, so the requirement-coverage and seam-disclosure conditions of the
gate are vacuous; every remaining condition holds. No auditor spawned per policy.

APPROVED. Branch specled_-f98/flaky-test-hardening (worktree specled_ex-flaky-test-hardening), commit 2a90f74 — not merged to main; this repo lands via PR + CI. Full merge gate green in the worktree: mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean, mix format --check-formatted ok, mix deps.unlock --check-unused ok, mix test 1029 tests + 3 properties 0 failures, mix spec.check (full, runs commands) status=pass errors=0 warnings=0 exit 0.

Observations 2 (SPECLED_SHOW_INFO hermeticity) and 4 (empty-artifact timeout budget) fixed. Observation 1/3 (tracer merge_edges property) is a critic-confirmed non-defect (sound pure-function property; likely a timeout artifact under load) — carried to standalone follow-up specled_-qvg for seed capture if it recurs. Closing per auditor sign-off. Ready for /open-pr.

Observation 2026-07-23 (from specled_-155.1 implementer, worktree specled_ex-store-v2-envelope @ 14a22d4): test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs failed once in a combined regression run, then passed 4 consecutive re-runs with different seeds and in isolation. :cover-state integration test (System.cmd child-BEAM); diff under test touched only Store file I/O + pure validation, so unrelated. Matches this ticket's signature class. Note for specled_-155 merge gates: if this file flakes at a gate, targeted re-run before flake-accept per protocol.

title: flaky one-off failures in full-suite/merged verification runs (2 observations 2026-07-02)
